output.summary: The CMS Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP) penalizes hospitals with higher-than-expected 30-day unplanned readmission rates for six conditions including acute myocardial infarction and heart failure. Payment reductions are capped at 3 percent and apply to all Medicare fee-for-service base operating DRG payments for the full fiscal year. Since FY2019 hospitals are grouped into five peer groups based on dual Medicare-Medicaid eligibility share and compared against their peer group median rather than a universal benchmark. For FY2025 approximately 2400 hospitals face penalties with about 7 percent facing reductions of 1 percent or more.
preserved_facts: Six covered conditions: acute myocardial infarction, heart failure, pneumonia, COPD, elective primary total hip/knee arthroplasty, and CABG surgery
Penalties apply to all Medicare fee-for-service base operating DRG payments for the full fiscal year (October 1 – September 30)
Since FY2019 hospitals are grouped into five peer groups based on proportion of dually eligible Medicare-Medicaid patients
Performance compared against peer group median rather than a universal benchmark
FY2025: approximately 2400 hospitals face some penalty
About 7 percent of penalized hospitals face reductions of 1 percent or more
71.6 percent of penalized hospitals receive penalties of less than 1 percent
FY2025 penalties based on claims data from July 2020 through June 2023 (three-year rolling period)
Hospitals receive confidential Hospital-Specific Reports and have 30 days to review and contest calculations
